steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim states that Reckoning and Resilience is distinguished from prior exhibitions (like Across County Lines, a photography-only show) by spanning multiple artistic disciplines. The correct choice must provide evidence of multiple disciplines.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Johnson is a North Carolina photographer, as were all of the artists featured in Across County Lines.’: Shows only a single discipline among the featured artists — does not prove breadth. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘The Nasher Museum has a world-class collection of contemporary art that includes many paintings and textile pieces from artists both inside and outside North Carolina.’: Addresses a factor unrelated to the range of disciplines in the exhibition. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘McIver is a painter, Johnson is a photographer, and Cartwright is a textile artist.’: Identifies three distinct disciplines — painting, photography, and textiles — directly proving the exhibition spans multiple disciplines. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Reckoning and Resilience and Across County Lines were curated to emphasize works that are not owned by the Nasher Museum.’: Addresses a factor unrelated to the exhibition's disciplinary scope. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
